rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2022 Line 1, DEFENDANT DALIA VILCHEZ'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Additionally, if service was made by regular mail, the notice would be insufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FELL HOLDINGS LLC VS. FELL ST AUTOMOTIVE CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Fell Holdings Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Unseal Record And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Breach Of Stipulation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of M. Ryan Pinkston
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2022 Line 2, PLAINTIFF FELL HOLDINGS LLC'S Motion To Unseal Record And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Breach Of Stipulation is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. CCP 1013b(b)(1). Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Decorius Boglin (Sued As Decorius Blogin)'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 14, 2022 Line 5, DEFENDANT DECORIUS BOGLIN'S Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly fill out sections 7, 10 and 16 of the form complaint and to attach a copy of the operative Notice. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from Notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Defendant to present an order tracking the tentative ruling at the hearing for execution. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
